verdict_reasoning: SRC (proto-oncogene tyrosine-protein kinase Src) is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase. It is anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation — both modifications anchor it to the cytoplasmic face only. Its kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains all face the cytoplasm. No extracellular domain exists. Checking all 5 contextual buckets: (1) no documented cancer outer-leaflet ecto-pool for the protein body; (2) no documented cell-death outer-leaflet exposure; (3) not a germline/developmental-restricted surface protein; (4) activation-induced display is inner-leaflet recruitment, not outer-leaflet; (5) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering exposing any domain extracellularly. No clinical antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets SRC on the outer leaflet. pMHC is not claimed. Verdict: no / inner_leaflet_anchored.
